name: Recover it!
input:
The first line of the input contains one integer $n$ ($1 \le n \le 2 \cdot 10^5$) — the number of elements in $a$.
The second line of the input contains $2n$ integers $b_1, b_2, \dots, b_{2n}$ ($2 \le b_i \le 2750131$), where $b_i$ is the $i$-th element of $b$. $2750131$ is the $199999$-th prime number.
output: In the only line of the output print $n$ integers $a_1, a_2, \dots, a_n$ ($2 \le a_i \le 2 \cdot 10^5$) in any order — the array $a$ from which the array $b$ can be obtained using the sequence of moves given in the problem statement. If there are multiple answers, you can print any.
